Running agents with --dangerously-skip-permissions is scary on a bare host; hand-rolling sandboxing, network policy, credential isolation, and audit logging per project is a lot of undifferentiated security work.

Building a persistent, autonomous coworker agent (not just chat) requires session state, resumable tool interrupts, scheduled check-ins, and delivery to external channels — this bundles those primitives for Genkit Go users.
